title: Bird gatherings: apply for a specific licence for poultry or other captive birds (passeriformes, columbiformes and ratites)
body: "Due to the current bird flu risk levels, there is a higher than usual risk to poultry and other captive birds. This means that it is harder to avoid (mitigate) the risks associated with gatherings and therefore less likely that APHA will be able to issue specific licences.  Before you apply for this licence you should:    read the rules on bird gathering in England to check if you can apply for this specific licence  check you will be able to meet the licence conditions at your event     Download and complete the licence application form.   Email it to APHA at outbreak.licensing@apha.gov.uk at least 20 days before your event. Include a copy of your contingency plan. You can apply for a licence further in advance.   APHA will consider your application and any risks associated with the proposed gathering based on the information you give in your licence application.   APHA will contact you by email to tell you the outcome of your application.      If the risks can be mitigated (reduced), APHA may give you an agreement in principle. This means you may be able to hold your gathering, but it does not guarantee it.  APHA will reassess the risks 7 days before your event and grant you the licence if the risks can still be mitigated. APHA will grant you the licence no more than 7 days before the gathering is due to take place.  If the risks cannot be mitigated, APHA will reject your application.  Get help   If you need help completing the form, contact the APHA Licensing team.   Email: outbreak.licensing@apha.gov.uk  "
